featured_promo.title: What I Learned at Fair Housing Day 2026 (and Why You Should Care)
featured_promo.body: Harv spent April 14 at UC Berkeley for the 5th Annual C.A.R. Fair Housing Day — seven sessions, three hundred REALTORS in Pauley Ballroom. He rebuilt economist Oscar Wei's 11-slide closing deck as interactive charts you can click through in about ten minutes.
featured_promo.quote: Black first-time buyers in California were 4 percent in 2010. In 2025, they are still 4 percent. Fifteen years of attention, zero movement at the entry point.
featured_promo.quote_attribution: — Oscar Wei, Deputy Chief Economist, C.A.R.

stocks.note: Numbers as of market close April 15th.
stocks.news:
NASDAQ led again as Tesla jumped 7.6% after unveiling its AI5 chip — Musk says it delivers 8–10x the muscle of the prior AI4 and rivals Nvidia's H100. TSMC and Samsung are slated to manufacture, with production targeted for 2027.

Amazon went shopping in space — agreeing to acquire satellite operator Globalstar for nearly $12 billion to bolster its direct-to-phone Leo service. Apple inked a parallel deal to bring Leo connectivity to iPhones and Apple Watches. SpaceX's Starlink still dominates with 10,000+ satellites; Amazon sits around 240 — but the gap is closing fast.

economy.commentary:
Kevin Warsh — Trump's pick to replace Jerome Powell — heads to the Senate Banking Committee soon. Powell's term ends May 15, and Powell himself reiterated the Fed remains "well positioned to wait" on rate moves, with Cleveland Fed President Hammack echoing the same.

Tariff refund checks start rolling out April 20. After the Supreme Court struck down the Trump tariff plan in February, at least 56,000 of the 330,000 affected businesses — including Costco, Kohl's, FedEx, and Nintendo — are first in line for reimbursement.

crypto.commentary:
Goldman Sachs and BlackRock are both building new Bitcoin ETFs, with Goldman expected to launch its first soon. If institutional money flows in, expect lower volatility across BTC and the broader crypto basket.

Pakistan ended its 8-year ban on banks working with crypto firms. Licensed crypto companies can now open bank accounts and process payments — a major opening in one of the world's largest retail crypto markets, where millions had been transacting peer-to-peer.

real_estate.homebuilder: Mortgage rates held flat for a fourth straight day — both 30 and 15-year unchanged as the 10-year yield hovers near 4.28%.
real_estate.commentary:
Total inventory across our 15-city tracker climbed to 2,629 — 233 coming soon and 220 new listings as the spring market continues to widen.

📍 Fremont: 320 total with 23 new listings and 52 coming soon — a small bump in active count.

📍 Oakland: 745 total with 46 new listings — still the Bay's deepest inventory pool by a wide margin.

📍 Hayward: 216 total with 12 new and 15 coming soon.

📍 Livermore: 209 total with 24 new listings — a notable surge of fresh activity.

📍 San Ramon: 177 total with 17 new and 20 coming soon — pipeline still full.

📍 Dublin: 162 total with 16 new listings.

📍 Pleasanton: 125 total with 14 new and 16 coming soon.

📍 Union City: 79 total — still one of the tightest markets on the board.

Industry note: Home equity (HELOC) rates are sitting near 7% — the lowest level in three years. With first-lien rates still in the low-6s, more owners are tapping equity to renovate rather than refinance or move, which is part of what's holding resale supply down.

Demographic note: NAR data shows Boomers (61–79) made up 42% of buyers and 55% of sellers in 2026. Millennials are still the largest owner cohort — they're just sitting on equity and not selling.
